refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and the system to achieve and maintain energy efficiency performance such that measured energy_per_ton (energy_used / ton_moved) does not exceed the customer-defined E_target over the agreed evaluation period, under nominal operating conditions and across the range of supported worksite scenarios.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-003 as stated is a KPI support requirement but lacks specificity on measurement scope, boundary conditions, and operational context. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is a measurable outcome dependent on operator behavior, vehicle efficiency, and system-level optimization. The requirement now explicitly bounds the evaluation period and scenario scope, making it verifiable and traceable to design decisions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Establish baseline energy consumption and ton-moved metrics via telemetry collection during controlled field trials. Compare measured energy_per_ton against E_target using statistical methods (e.g., confidence intervals) to confirm compliance. Verification requires instrumentation of energy sources (fuel, electric) and load/distance tracking across representative worksite conditions.
sub_frs: FR-3.1: The NGHE solution shall measure and report energy consumption (fuel or electric) with ±5% accuracy.
FR-3.2: The NGHE solution shall measure and report work output (ton_moved) with ±5% accuracy.
FR-3.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback to the operator to enable behavioral optimization.
FR-3.4: The NGHE solution shall log energy and work metrics for post-session analysis and KPI validation.
FR-3.5: The NGHE solution shall support tele-operator energy-aware task planning and execution to minimize energy per ton.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement energy metering hardware (fuel flow sensor, electric power monitor) integrated with vehicle CAN bus; couple with real-time efficiency calculation and operator display; log all metrics to onboard storage and transmit via telemetry.
DP-B: Equip vehicle with passive fuel consumption estimation based on engine load and RPM; derive work output from GPS and load cell; calculate efficiency offline post-mission.

DP-C: Integrate energy monitoring at the NGHE solution level (centralized telemetry aggregation); implement efficiency calculation in cloud backend; push feedback to operator via Telemetry interface; enable tele-operator to view and optimize task sequencing.

DP-D: Combine onboard energy metering (DP-A) with cloud-based analytics (DP-C); enable real-time feedback to operator and tele-operator; support predictive energy optimization via machine learning on historical mission data.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid onboard + cloud energy efficiency architecture. Implement direct energy metering hardware (fuel flow, electric power sensors) on the Vehicle with CAN bus integration for real-time accuracy. Transmit raw energy and work metrics via Telemetry interface to NGHE Solution backend. Perform efficiency calculation and analytics in cloud; push real-time feedback to operator display and tele-operator dashboard. Enable machine learning-based energy optimization recommendations based on mission history, worksite conditions, and operator behavior patterns.

potential_sources: Energy metering hardware selection couples to vehicle power architecture (electrical/fuel system design).
Real-time feedback display couples to operator interface design and HMI standards.
Telemetry transmission couples to Network Link bandwidth and latency requirements.
Cloud analytics couples to data storage, processing latency, and cybersecurity policies.
Machine learning model training couples to historical mission data availability and labeling effort.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is moderately coupled but couplings are well-understood and manageable. The hybrid approach decouples onboard metering (deterministic, safety-critical) from cloud analytics (non-critical, optimizable). Recommend explicit interface contracts between Vehicle energy sensors and NGHE Solution telemetry ingestion to manage coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Energy metering hardware (fuel flow, power sensors) can be integrated into vehicle CAN bus with <2% measurement error and <100ms latency.
Assumption: Telemetry bandwidth supports continuous transmission of energy + work metrics at ≥1 Hz sampling rate.
Assumption: Cloud backend can process and return efficiency feedback within <5 seconds of metric arrival (acceptable for operator guidance).
Risk: Sensor calibration drift over mission duration may degrade accuracy; mitigation: implement in-mission calibration checks and alert operator if confidence drops below threshold.
Risk: Tele-operator energy optimization recommendations may conflict with safety constraints or real-time task demands; mitigation: Safety Services must validate all recommendations before display.

Risk: Machine learning model may overfit to historical data and fail on novel worksite conditions; mitigation: implement model validation on held-out test set and continuous retraining with new mission data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the acceptable latency for real-time feedback (currently assumed <5s). If operator feedback must be <1s, consider moving efficiency calculation to onboard Vehicle module rather than cloud. Also specify the minimum historical mission dataset required to train ML models (e.g., 100 missions, 10 worksites) to bound project schedule and data collection effort.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level energy efficiency orchestration, including telemetry ingestion, cloud analytics, efficiency calculation, feedback generation, and integration with Teleoperation and Safety Services. Owns the energy KPI measurement, reporting, and optimization logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: raw energy consumption (fuel flow, electric power) and work output (ton_moved, distance) metrics from Vehicle. Outbound: efficiency feedback, recommendations, and KPI status to operator display and tele-operator dashboard.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: task definitions and sequencing from tele-operator; NGHE Solution uses task context to optimize energy efficiency recommendations (e.g., suggest task reordering to minimize energy per ton).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: safety constraints and operational limits from Safety Services. Outbound: energy efficiency recommendations validated against safety constraints before display to operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; supports telemetry transmission, feedback delivery, and task coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment: energy efficiency KPI measurement, analytics, and feedback generation are core NGHE Solution responsibilities. Diffusion risk: telemetry ingestion and cloud backend infrastructure may be shared with other NGHE Solution functions (e.g., safety monitoring, predictive maintenance); recommend clear API boundaries to minimize coupling.

Moderate containment: Vehicle owns energy metering hardware and CAN bus integration (physical sensors, data acquisition). However, efficiency calculation and optimization logic should NOT be in Vehicle; this creates tight coupling to vehicle firmware and limits cloud-based ML optimization. Recommend Vehicle expose raw energy/work metrics via standard interface; NGHE Solution owns analytics.

Moderate-low containment: Teleoperation module displays efficiency feedback and receives optimization recommendations, but does not own the calculation or analytics logic. Risk: if efficiency feedback is tightly coupled to tele-operator UI, changes to analytics will require UI rework. Recommend Teleoperation consume efficiency data via standard Telemetry interface.

Low-moderate containment: Safety Services validates energy efficiency recommendations against safety constraints (e.g., do not recommend aggressive acceleration if it violates stability limits). However, Safety Services should not own the efficiency calculation itself. Recommend Safety Services expose constraint APIs; NGHE Solution calls these APIs before displaying recommendations.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of energy efficiency KPI measurement and optimization because: (1) it has system-wide visibility of energy consumption, work output, and operational context; (2) it can integrate data from Vehicle (sensors), Teleoperation (task context), and Safety Services (constraints); (3) it can implement cloud-based analytics and ML without tight coupling to vehicle firmware or operator UI; (4) it can evolve optimization algorithms independently of vehicle hardware or tele-operator interface changes.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution telemetry ingestion is not well-isolated, energy metric processing may interfere with other NGHE Solution functions (e.g., safety monitoring, predictive maintenance). Mitigation: implement separate data pipelines and processing threads for energy metrics.

Risk: Vehicle energy metering hardware may not be standardized across vehicle types (e.g., different fuel systems, electric architectures). Mitigation: define a Vehicle-to-NGHE Solution energy metric interface specification; Vehicle module is responsible for normalizing raw sensor data to this interface.

Risk: Tele-operator may not have sufficient context to understand or act on energy efficiency recommendations in real-time. Mitigation: Teleoperation module should provide decision support (e.g., highlight recommended task reordering) rather than raw efficiency numbers.

Risk: Cloud backend latency or outage may prevent real-time feedback to operator. Mitigation: implement fallback onboard efficiency calculation (simplified, non-ML) in Vehicle module; operator receives basic feedback even if cloud is unavailable.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the Vehicle-to-NGHE Solution energy metric interface (schema, sampling rate, accuracy requirements). Specify the NGHE Solution-to-Teleoperation feedback interface (what efficiency data is pushed, how often, in what format). Clarify the NGHE Solution-to-Safety Services constraint validation API (what constraints are checked, how are conflicts resolved). These interface specifications will reduce diffusion risk and enable parallel development of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid onboard + cloud energy efficiency architecture) directly addresses the refined FR by implementing end-to-end energy measurement, calculation, feedback, and optimization. The DP specifies how to achieve the energy_per_ton KPI through hardware metering, telemetry transmission, cloud analytics, and operator/tele-operator feedback. Coupling is moderate and manageable. Assumptions and risks are documented and mitigable.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Energy metering hardware integration in Vehicle may couple to vehicle firmware updates; recommend versioned interface contract.
Diffusion risk: Cloud analytics backend in NGHE Solution may be shared with other functions (safety, maintenance); recommend separate data pipelines and processing threads.
Diffusion risk: Telemetry interface carries both energy metrics and other NGHE Solution data; recommend clear message schemas and versioning to prevent coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of energy efficiency KPI logic. Vehicle owns metering hardware and raw data acquisition. Teleoperation and Safety Services consume efficiency feedback and constraints respectively. Interfaces are well-defined (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Containment scores support this allocation: NGHE Solution 0.92, Vehicle 0.75, Teleoperation 0.60, Safety Services 0.50.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-003 is feasible via field trials with instrumented vehicles. Telemetry data collection enables post-mission KPI calculation. Acceptance criteria (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) can be validated statistically over the agreed evaluation period. Recommend establishing baseline metrics before optimization to measure improvement.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution is responsible for energy efficiency KPI measurement and reporting. Vehicle provides raw energy and work metrics. Teleoperation provides task context. Safety Services provides operational constraints. Each module has a well-defined role and interface. Ownership boundaries are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Define the Vehicle-to-NGHE Solution energy metric interface specification (schema, sampling rate, accuracy, latency requirements).
Specify the acceptable latency for real-time efficiency feedback to operator (currently assumed <5s; if <1s required, reconsider cloud vs. onboard calculation).
Clarify the minimum historical mission dataset required to train ML models (e.g., 100 missions, 10 worksites) to bound project schedule.
Document the NGHE Solution-to-Safety Services constraint validation API (what constraints are checked, how are conflicts resolved).
Establish a fallback onboard efficiency calculation in Vehicle module for cloud outage scenarios.

decision.justification: The refined FR-NGHE-003 and selected DP-D are well-aligned and feasible. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution as primary owner, with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services as collaborators) is appropriate and supported by containment analysis. However, the refined FR has been decomposed into five sub-FRs (FR-3.1 through FR-3.5) that require further design elaboration. Each sub-FR addresses a specific aspect of energy efficiency (measurement, feedback, logging, tele-operator optimization) and must be individually designed and allocated to implementation modules. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify interface contracts, reduce diffusion risk, and enable parallel development.
decision.justification_detail: The current level establishes the system-level energy efficiency architecture (hybrid onboard + cloud). The next level must address: (1) energy metering hardware selection and CAN bus integration (FR-3.1, FR-3.2); (2) real-time feedback display and HMI design (FR-3.3); (3) data logging and post-mission analysis (FR-3.4); (4) tele-operator task optimization (FR-3.5). Each of these sub-FRs will have distinct DPs and IM allocations, and will expose interface requirements that must be managed at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-3.1: The NGHE solution shall measure and report energy consumption (fuel or electric) with ±5% accuracy.
FR-3.2: The NGHE solution shall measure and report work output (ton_moved) with ±5% accuracy.
FR-3.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback to the operator to enable behavioral optimization.
FR-3.4: The NGHE solution shall log energy and work metrics for post-session analysis and KPI validation.
FR-3.5: The NGHE solution shall support tele-operator energy-aware task planning and execution to minimize energy per ton.

assumptions: Energy metering hardware (fuel flow, power sensors) can be integrated into vehicle CAN bus with <2% measurement error and <100ms latency.
Telemetry bandwidth supports continuous transmission of energy + work metrics at ≥1 Hz sampling rate.
Cloud backend can process and return efficiency feedback within <5 seconds of metric arrival.
Operator feedback latency of <5s is acceptable for behavioral optimization; if <1s required, onboard calculation is necessary.
Historical mission dataset of ≥100 missions across ≥10 worksites is available or will be collected during field trials.
Safety Services can validate energy efficiency recommendations within <1 second.
Vehicle module can expose raw energy and work metrics via a standardized interface without firmware changes.
open_risks: Sensor calibration drift over mission duration may degrade accuracy; mitigation: implement in-mission calibration checks.
Tele-operator energy optimization recommendations may conflict with safety constraints or real-time task demands; mitigation: Safety Services validation.
Machine learning model may overfit to historical data and fail on novel worksite conditions; mitigation: model validation and continuous retraining.
Cloud backend latency or outage may prevent real-time feedback; mitigation: fallback onboard efficiency calculation.
Energy metering hardware may not be standardized across vehicle types; mitigation: define Vehicle-to-NGHE Solution interface specification.
Telemetry interface may become congested if energy metrics are not efficiently encoded; mitigation: define message schemas and compression strategies.
evidence_needed: Energy metering hardware specifications and CAN bus integration feasibility study.
Telemetry bandwidth analysis: confirm ≥1 Hz sampling rate is achievable with current Network Link capacity.
Cloud backend latency measurements: confirm <5s feedback latency under nominal load.
Historical mission dataset: collect or identify ≥100 missions across ≥10 worksites for ML model training.
Safety Services constraint validation API specification and performance testing.
Operator HMI mockup and usability testing for real-time efficiency feedback.
Fallback onboard efficiency calculation design and testing for cloud outage scenarios.
